title: Rosa × centifolia bullata
intro: Rosa × centifolia bullata, also known as Bullata Rose, is a fragrant, climbing rose species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique, compact growth habit and abundant, fragrant flowers. The health benefits of Rosa × centifolia bullata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve mood, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, due to its calming and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly geraniol and linalool, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rosa × centifolia bullata include rose tea, rose oil, and rose petal extracts, which are used in traditional medicine for their therapeutic and cosmetic applications.
taxonomy: Rosa × centifolia bullata is classified as a type of shrub, specifically a cultivar within the genus Rosa, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a variant of Rosa centifolia, also known as the Cabbage Rose. This classification suggests that Rosa × centifolia bullata is a hybrid of Rosa centifolia and possibly another species within the Rosa genus. Further classification may involve identifying the specific species used in its hybridization. The classification of Rosa × centifolia bullata as a shrub within the Rosaceae family highlights its botanical origins.
uses: Rosa × centifolia bullata is a type of fragrant rose. Rosa × centifolia bullata flowers are often used as a source of essential oil, which has been utilized in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The petals of this rose can be used to create a soothing tea that may help with digestive issues and as a natural remedy for menstrual cramps. The essential oil extracted from Rosa × centifolia bullata flowers has also been used to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Additionally, the oil has been used to aid in the treatment of anxiety and insomnia due to its calming effects.
benefits: Rosa × centifolia bullata is a fragrant, double-petaled rose variety known for its distinctive appearance and potential health benefits. The antioxidants present in Rosa × centifolia bullata may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The essential oil extracted from the flowers of this rose variety may possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. Furthermore, the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in Rosa × centifolia bullata may have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Rosa × centifolia bullata.
properties: Rosa × centifolia bullata is known to have various therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat skin issues such as acne, wounds, and eczema. Its therapeutic properties are also believed to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health and immune system function. The plant's antioxidant properties may also help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Rosa × centifolia bullata is used in traditional medicine to treat various other health issues.
constituents: Rosa × centifolia bullata. The active constituents of Rosa × centifolia bullata, also known as the double English rose or Provence rose, include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The essential oils in this plant are primarily composed of geraniol, linalool, and citronellol, which contribute to its fragrance and potential therapeutic properties. Flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin are also present, providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These active constituents may be responsible for the plant's traditional uses in perfumery and herbal medicine.
